title: Object Properties
content:
### Working with Properties

Objects are **mutable** -- you can add, change, and delete properties freely.

---

### Adding Properties

```js
let user = { name: "John" };
user.age = 30;          // Added
user["city"] = "NYC";  // Added
```

### Changing Properties

```js
user.age = 31; // Changed
```

### Deleting Properties

```js
delete user.city; // Removes the city property
```

---

### Checking if a Property Exists

```js
let user = { name: "John", age: 30 };

console.log("name" in user);  // true
console.log(user.hasOwnProperty("name")); // true
console.log(user.phone !== undefined); // false (property doesn't exist)
```

---

### Listing Properties

```js
let user = { name: "John", age: 30, city: "NYC" };

console.log(Object.keys(user));   // ["name", "age", "city"]
console.log(Object.values(user)); // ["John", 30, "NYC"]
console.log(Object.entries(user)); // [["name","John"], ["age",30], ["city","NYC"]]
```

---

### Computed Property Names

You can use **expressions** as property keys:

```js
let key = "dynamicKey";
let obj = {
  [key]: "Dynamic Value"
};
console.log(obj.dynamicKey); // "Dynamic Value"
```

> **Property keys** are always strings (or Symbols). If you use a non-string, it gets converted to a string.

quiz.question: How do you remove a property from an object?
